name: Common beet / red beetroot (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw root, Beta vulgaris subsp. vulgaris)

components.name: water
components.evidence: USDA proximate; beet root is ~87-88% water

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 169145 total sugars 6.76 g x 94.8% sucrose (Wruss/Nemzer beetroot juice profile; Sci food-comp)
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon source: red beet carbohydrate is >90% sucrose; roots are 94.8% sucrose of total free sugar

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 169145 total sugars 6.76 g x 3.3% glucose
components.evidence: minor free sugar in root (3.3% of total sugars)

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: USDA FDC 169145 total sugars 6.76 g x 1.9% fructose
components.evidence: minor free sugar in root (1.9% of total sugars)

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Sady et al. 2025, Plants 14(4):591 (Beta vulgaris root organic acids) doi:10.3390/plants14040591
components.evidence: Reported as the DOMINANT root organic acid (4.29-15.0 g/100g dw); midpoint 9.6 dw x ~0.135 dry-matter = ~1.3 g/100g fw. Cultivar-dependent, treat as approximate/high estimate

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence: midpoint 1.09 dw x 0.135 = 0.147 g/100g fw; a defining anti-nutritive acid of beet

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: present in trace amounts only; unquantified

components.name: nitrate
components.source: Hord et al. 2009 Am J Clin Nutr 90:1-10 (beet classified 'very high nitrate', >250 mg/100g); range 110-400 mg/100g
components.evidence: MAJOR defining metabolite of beetroot; a preferred N source; not in FooDB profile

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: beet is one of the richest dietary betaine sources; usable N/methyl donor and osmolyte

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: most abundant amino acid (Glu+Gln); beet root notably high in free glutamine/glutamate

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: corrected from D-serine in FooDB profile (protein-derived AA is L)

components.name: iron
components.evidence: single consolidated iron value (Fe2+/Fe3+ compartment duplicates removed)

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: provitamin A; trace in red beet

components.name: total lipid
components.evidence: beet root is essentially fat-free; not a growth-relevant lipid source

remove.name: Alpha-Carotene
remove.reason: not measured in red beet (a betalain, not carotenoid, vegetable); USDA lists no alpha-carotene; FooDB noise

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed vitamin A (retinol) does not occur in plants; beet vitamin A is provitamin-A carotenoid only

remove.name: D-Galactose
remove.reason: free galactose is not a documented beet root sugar (root sugar is 94.8% sucrose, 3.3% glucose, 1.9% fructose); FooDB noise

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: duplicate/compartment-specific iron entry; consolidated into single 'iron' value

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: BiGG compartment artifact, not a food-composition entry; consolidated into 'iron'

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a beet growth nutrient

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: cobalt only enters biology via B12, which plants lack; non-nutritive trace

remove.name: Fluoride
remove.reason: trace, not quantified for beet by USDA; not a growth nutrient

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: metabolic gas / boundary metabolite, not a food nutrient of the medium

remove.name: Alpha-Tocopherol duplicate note
remove.reason: retained once as alpha-tocopherol (vitamin E); no change, listed for clarity

missing_added: sucrose (with amount) - the dominant carbon source, previously only listed generically
fumaric acid (all organic acids were absent)
keep_uncovered: betanin / betacyanins (defining red betalain pigments; genuine, no BiGG met)
Luteolin (genuine flavone, low; from FooDB uncovered)
Quercetin (genuine but low in beet; kept as uncovered phytochemical, no reliable amount)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169145 (Beets, raw) - proximates, sugars, amino acids, minerals, vitamins, betaine, choline
Sady et al. 2025, Plants 14(4):591, doi:10.3390/plants14040591 - Beta vulgaris root organic acids and free sugars
Wruss et al. 2015, J Food Comp Anal 42:46-55, doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2015.03.005 - beetroot sugar (94.8% sucrose) and oxalic acid in juice
Hord, Tang & Bryan 2009, Am J Clin Nutr 90:1-10 - dietary nitrate content of vegetables (beet 'very high', >250 mg/100g)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is sucrose (~6.4 g/100g, >90% of sugars) with minor glucose+fructose; organic acids (succinic, oxalic, quinic, citric, malic) were entirely missing from the FooDB profile and are added here (succinic value is cultivar-dependent and treated as a high estimate). Nitrate (~250 mg/100g) is a defining beet metabolite and a preferred N source; betaine (129 mg/100g) is an abundant osmolyte/methyl donor. Organic-acid amounts converted from dry weight using ~13.5% dry matter and should be read as fresh-weight approximations.
